steps.title: Link the incident to the idea
steps.content: The underlined sentence tells of Liang imagining a trip to New York before later seeing New York in reality. The following sentence calls this an "incident" that leads to a reflection about imagination foreshadowing reality.

steps.title: Choose the function of the event
steps.content: A shifts to writing success, which is not the later reflection. B falsely suggests Liang could not distinguish reality from fantasy. C focuses on adventure rather than the stated phenomenon. The sentence functions as an event that exemplifies the broader reflection. The answer is D.
